description:
Change the group of a local interactive users files and directories to a group that the interactive user is a member of. To change the group owner of a local interactive users files and directories, use the following command: 
$ sudo chgrp USER_GROUP /home/USER/FILE_DIR
This rule ensures every file or directory under the home directory related to an interactive user is group-owned by an interactive user.
rationale:
If a local interactive users files are group-owned by a group of which the
user is not a member, unintended users may be able to access them.
ocil:
To verify all files and directories in interactive user home directory are group-owned by a group the user is a member of, run the following command: 
$ sudo ls -lLR /home/USER

warnings.general:
Due to OVAL limitation, this rule can report a false negative in a
specific situation where two interactive users swap the group-ownership
of folders or files in their respective home directories.

title: All User Files and Directories In The Home Directory Must Be Group-Owned By The Primary Group
